message:
br-bd-c88jg FS_IOC_FSSETXATTR ioctl: chattr -p / xfs_io fsxattr write parity

Companion to bd-6wzug (FS_IOC_FSGETXATTR / read side). Userspace
passes a 28-byte struct fsxattr through the SET ioctl; ext4 must
project xflags back onto i_flags, persist projid, and reject the
fields it does not implement (extsize, cowextsize, HASATTR sentinel,
unknown FS_XFLAG_* bits).

ffs-core surface:
- New `FsOps::set_inode_fsxattr` trait method (default returns
  UnsupportedFeature).
- OpenFs override: rejects extsize/cowextsize != 0 with EINVAL,
  reads current i_flags, calls the new `xflags_to_ext4_flags`
  helper, then dispatches through `set_inode_flags` so the existing
  EXT4_USER_SETTABLE_FLAGS gate, EXT4_COMPR_FL feature check, and
  journal-aware write path stay in one place. Project ID is
  applied directly via `ffs_inode::write_inode` only when it
  differs from the on-disk value.
- `xflags_to_ext4_flags(xflags, current_flags)`:
  * Rejects FS_XFLAG_HASATTR with EINVAL (kernel-set, read-only).
  * Rejects unknown bits (REALTIME, NODEFRAG, DAX, etc.) with
    EOPNOTSUPP — matches Linux's ext4_fileattr_set rejection.
  * Maps the supported subset (IMMUTABLE, APPEND, SYNC, NOATIME,
    NODUMP, PROJINHERIT) onto their EXT4_*_FL counterparts while
    preserving non-user-settable bits in `current_flags`.

ffs-fuse wiring:
- New `FS_IOC_FSSETXATTR` constant (0x401c5820, _IOW('X', 32)) +
  `FS_IOC_FSSETXATTR_SIZE = 28`.
- `parse_fsxattr_request` decodes the 28-byte LE payload, returns
  EINVAL on short buffer, zeroes fsx_nextents (kernel-set).
- dispatch_ioctl branch: read-only mount -> EROFS; parse -> EINVAL
  on bad shape; ops.set_inode_fsxattr; commit_request_scope.
- IoctlRecordingFs gains a SetFsxattr trace + override.

Tests: 689 ffs-core + 259 ffs-fuse pass. New regressions:
- ffs-core: round-trips xflags + projid through SET/GET.
- ffs-core: extsize and cowextsize != 0 surface EINVAL.
- ffs-core: HASATTR sentinel rejected with EINVAL.
- ffs-core: unknown xflag (FS_XFLAG_REALTIME) rejected with
  EOPNOTSUPP.
- ffs-core: clearing all xflags removes HASATTR too.
- ffs-fuse: 28-byte payload parses + routes to FsOps with
  Commit in trace.
- ffs-fuse: 27-byte payload -> EINVAL.
- ffs-fuse: read-only mount -> EROFS.

fmt + clippy clean via rch CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenfs_cc_bd_c88jg.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
